concerns.text: Task T004 states 'Constitution Principle VI has been amended' as a comment, but no task exists to perform the amendment. The Constitution (Principle VI) currently mandates 'analytical homogenization formulas'. Executing T004 (FFT-based homogenization) without a prior task to amend the Constitution violates the project's legal constraints (FR-030) and creates a governance blocker.

Task T013 implements 128x128 pixel images. Spec FR-001 and US-1 Acceptance Scenario 1 explicitly mandate '256x256 pixel grayscale images'. The task silently relaxes the resolution requirement without a documented staging exception or spec amendment, violating the deliverable scope.

Task T027 implements 'One-way ANOVA and Tukey HSD'. Spec FR-007 and SC-004 explicitly mandate 'paired t-tests' to compare prediction errors across bins. Replacing the specified statistical method with ANOVA violates the functional requirement and success criterion definition.

Task T016 validates 'Voigt-Reuss-Hill bounds'. Spec SC-001 and US-2 Acceptance Scenario 2 require verifying the model achieves 'MAE ≤ 5%'. Validating physical plausibility (VRH bounds) is not equivalent to verifying the specific error threshold success criterion. The acceptance criterion is dropped.

Task T029a calculates degradation rate for densities > max_training_density. Spec SC-002 requires measuring generalization against the 'distribution of inclusion densities in the test set' and quantifying degradation for 'out-of-distribution densities'. The task assumes a specific threshold logic not explicitly defined in the spec, risking a mismatch in the definition of 'out-of-distribution' required for the success criterion.
